seoTitle: Attach a PDF to an Emailed Invoice in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Send contracts or receipts alongside your bills by learning how to attach and email PDFs with invoices in QuickBooks Online.
intro: Sometimes an invoice needs backup, like a contract, receipt, or timesheet, sent along with it. This guide shows how to attach a PDF to a QuickBooks Online invoice and make sure it's included when the bill is emailed to your client.

steps.heading: Open the target invoice
steps.body: Go to Sales, then Invoices, and open the specific billing document you want to attach a file to.

steps.heading: Find the attachments section
steps.body: Scroll to the bottom of the invoice form to locate the Attachments panel.

steps.heading: Upload your PDF
steps.body: Click Browse or drag and drop your PDF contract or receipt directly into the attachment area.

steps.heading: Enable email delivery
steps.body: Check the box labeled Attach to email next to the uploaded file so it's included when the invoice is sent.

steps.heading: Send the invoice
steps.body: Review the customer email and line items, then click Save and Send to deliver the invoice with the PDF attached.

faq.question: Do I have to manually enable the attachment every time I send?
faq.answer: You need to check the Attach to email box on each file unless you set a global default under Account and Settings.

faq.question: Can I attach more than one file to an invoice?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag multiple files into the attachments dock and choose which ones to include in the email.

faq.question: What file types can I attach?
faq.answer: PDFs are the most common and reliable for invoices, though QuickBooks accepts several common document formats.

faq.question: Will the attachment show up in the client's email or just the portal?
faq.answer: If you check Attach to email, the file is included directly in the emailed message, not just the online invoice link.
